page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n1 server family. It features 8 vCPUs and 30 GB of RAM, configured with an x86_64 architecture. The underlying hardware is powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz, offering 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 45 MB L3 cache, 32 KB L1d cache, and 0.25 MB L2 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA, while lacking AVX-512 capabilities. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower-to-average tiers. In CPU-heavy tasks, the server demonstrates weak single-core and multi-core performance in stress-ng tests, alongside a poor single-core Geekbench score. However, it achieves average ratings in Passmark CPU and memory tests, as well as average performance in ray tracing and database operations. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput for large block reads and writes, though small block cached reads remain weak. Cryptographic operations and LLM text generation for small models also perform at average levels, while web serving and Redis SET operations fall into the weak category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-standard-8 offers a balanced resource density suitable for legacy workloads or non-demanding general-purpose applications. Since it relies on older-generation Intel Xeon hardware, its performance-per-resource ratio is modest compared to newer architectures, making it a qualitative choice for steady-state workloads that do not require high-burst capabilities or advanced vector instructions. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, small database operations, development environments, and lightweight background processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 30 GB of RAM on an x86_64 architecture. Powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, it includes a 45 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instructions, though it lacks AVX-512. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data indicates weak to average performance, with average marks in Passmark CPU, database operations, and large-block memory bandwidth, but weaker results in single-core processing and static web serving. Given its balanced resource profile and older hardware class, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for steady-state, non-intensive workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, development environments, small databases, and basic web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering balanced resource density for steady-state workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose VM with 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for basic workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and moderate memory capacity for steady-state workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 30 GB RAM providing a balanced resource profile
Dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 4 physical cores
Average performance in Passmark CPU and database benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for development environments and small databases
